session.user: Run pytest on tests/commands/one_shot/ -v (the entire directory) and report results. Just run the command and report what passes/fails.
session.assistant: I'll run pytest on the tests/commands/one_shot/ directory and report the results.

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: Run pytest on entire one_shot test directory with verbose output

session.tool_result:
============================= test session starts ============================== platform linux -- Python 3.13.12, pytest-9.0.2, pluggy-1.6.0 rootdir: /home/runner/work/erk/erk configfile: pyproject.toml plugins: anyio-4.12.1, cov-7.0.0, xdist-3.8.0, asyncio-1.3.0 asyncio: mode=Mode.AUTO, debug=False, asyncio_default_fixture_loop_scope=function, asyncio_default_test_loop_scope=function collected 29 items
tests/commands/one_shot/test_branch_name.py .... [ 13%] tests/commands/one_shot/test_one_shot.py ............ [ 55%] tests/commands/one_shot/test_one_shot_dispatch.py ...... [ 75%] tests/commands/one_shot/test_one_shot_remote_dispatch.py ....... [100%]
============================== 29 passed in 0.82s ==============================
session.assistant:
pytest passed: All 29 tests in tests/commands/one_shot/ passed in 0.82s
**Summary:** - 4 test files collected - 29 tests total - 0 failures - 0 skipped
**Test breakdown:** - test_branch_name.py: 4 passed - test_one_shot.py: 12 passed - test_one_shot_dispatch.py: 6 passed - test_one_shot_remote_dispatch.py: 7 passed
